summary: Round-2 re-review of the test-only change adding Test_DiffZeroFilledCells. Both round-1 findings are resolved. The diff is now purely additive (git --numstat: 89 insertions, 0 deletions) — the incidental gofmt comment-realignment in Test_parseDuration is gone. The case-sensitive verify-mask concern is correctly handled as a documented deviation: the inline NOTE accurately states Go's -run is case-sensitive and points readers to -run '[Dd]iff' for full regression coverage, which I verified empirically (-run Diff selects only the new test; -run '[Dd]iff' selects and passes all four diff tests). The test itself remains well-constructed and locks tech-debt [007].

suggestions.suggestion: Round-1 major (verify mask `-run Diff` matches only the new test, not the lowercase siblings) is now an accurate, documented deviation rather than a silent gap. The in-code NOTE explains the case-sensitivity and directs to `-run '[Dd]iff'` for full regression coverage; verification runs both masks. The test name is intentionally kept as-is per the orchestrator's `-run Diff` constraint. Verified empirically: `-run Diff` -> Test_DiffZeroFilledCells only (PASS); `-run '[Dd]iff'` -> Test_diff, Test_DiffZeroFilledCells, Test_diff_pg18_wal_stats_age, Test_diffPair (all PASS). No further action required; noted for traceability only.
suggestions.benefit: The documented two-mask verification provides genuine regression coverage of the sibling diff tests while respecting the task constraint on the test name.
